hook: If proof verification is the real bottleneck, consensus cannot treat finality as independent of the proof pipeline. In the cited zkVerify-style setup, aggregation domains cap at 128 proofs with 16 pending aggregations, and when queues fill, CannotAggregate or DomainStorageFull halts new ingress until publication clears.
why_it_matters: If you are learning how zero-knowledge proof changes distributed consensus, this gives you a concrete lens for analyzing finality as a queueing and backpressure problem rather than just a voting rule.
action: Write a 10-line pseudocode state machine for checkpoint finality with three states—voted, proving, finalized—and one transition guard that blocks new checkpoints when aggregationSize or queueSize is saturated per zkVerify domain limits.
